calculate_rank_jaccard <- function(df_A, df_B, pval_thresh = 0.05, n=500) {
  
  # Ensure we are only looking at the common genes between both dataframes
  common_genes <- intersect(df_A$V1, df_B$V1)
  df_A <- df_A[df_A$V1 %in% common_genes, ]
  df_B <- df_B[df_B$V1 %in% common_genes, ]
  
  # Step 3: Incorporate the sign into the gene name
  # If logFC is negative, prepend a "-", otherwise keep the gene name
  signed_genes_A <- ifelse(df_A$logFC < 0, paste0("-", df_A$V1), df_A$V1)
  signed_genes_B <- ifelse(df_B$logFC < 0, paste0("-", df_B$V1), df_B$V1)
  
  # Step 2: Sort based on significance (smallest adj.P.Val at the top)
  order_A <- order(df_A$adj.P.Val, decreasing = FALSE)
  order_B <- order(df_B$adj.P.Val, decreasing = FALSE)
  
  ranked_A <- signed_genes_A[order_A]
  ranked_B <- signed_genes_B[order_B]
  
  # Re-order the p-values so we can use them to find 'n'
  sorted_pval_A <- df_A$adj.P.Val[order_A]
  sorted_pval_B <- df_B$adj.P.Val[order_B]
  
  # Step 5 (part 1): Determine n
  # Set n to include all genes in A and B with a p-value < 0.05. 
  # We take the maximum count between the two lists to be inclusive.
  n_A <- sum(sorted_pval_A < pval_thresh, na.rm = TRUE)
  n_B <- sum(sorted_pval_B < pval_thresh, na.rm = TRUE)
  
  # Safety checks
  if (n == 0) return(0) 
  n <- min(n, length(ranked_A)) # Prevent n from exceeding total genes
  
  top_A <- ranked_A[1:n]
  top_B <- ranked_B[1:n]
  
  # 2. Find genes that appear in BOTH top n lists
  common_top <- intersect(top_A, top_B)
  
  # 3. Find the rank (index) of these common genes in both lists
  rank_in_A <- match(common_top, top_A)
  rank_in_B <- match(common_top, top_B)
  
  # 4. A gene enters the intersection at the maximum of its two ranks
  entry_step <- pmax(rank_in_A, rank_in_B)
  
  # 5. Count how many genes enter at each step (from 1 to n)
  # Using factor() ensures that steps with 0 new genes are still counted as 0
  entry_counts <- table(factor(entry_step, levels = 1:n))
  
  # 6. The intersection size at step i is just the cumulative sum of entries
  intersect_len_vec <- cumsum(entry_counts)
  
  # 7. Calculate union length and Jaccard index for all steps simultaneously
  i_vec <- 1:n
  union_len_vec <- (2 * i_vec) - intersect_len_vec
  ji_vec <- intersect_len_vec / union_len_vec
  
  # 8. Sum it all up
  ji_sum <- sum(ji_vec)
  
  return(ji_vec)
}
# Compare Jax vs UCSF for each condition
jax_JAX_MSK  <- calculate_rank_jaccard(Jax_JAX, Jax_MSK)
jax_JAX_UCSF  <- calculate_rank_jaccard(Jax_JAX, Jax_UCSF)
jax_JAX_NW <- calculate_rank_jaccard(Jax_JAX, Jax_NW)
jax_MSK_UCSF  <- calculate_rank_jaccard(Jax_MSK, Jax_UCSF)
jax_MSK_NW  <- calculate_rank_jaccard(Jax_MSK, Jax_NW)
jax_UCSF_NW <- calculate_rank_jaccard(Jax_UCSF, Jax_NW)

# # Print the results
# cat("Rank-Weighted Jaccard Scores for JAX:\n")
# cat("JAX vs. MSK: ", jax_JAX_MSK, "\n")
# cat("JAX vs. UCSF: ", jax_JAX_UCSF, "\n")
# cat("JAX vs. NW:", jax_JAX_NW, "\n")
# cat("MSK vs. UCSF:  ", jax_MSK_UCSF, "\n")
# cat("MSK vs. NW: ", jax_MSK_NW, "\n")
# cat("UCSF vs. NW: ", jax_UCSF_NW, "\n")

# Compare Jax vs UCSF for each condition
ucsf_JAX_MSK  <- calculate_rank_jaccard(Ucsf_JAX, Ucsf_MSK)
ucsf_JAX_UCSF  <- calculate_rank_jaccard(Ucsf_JAX, Ucsf_UCSF)
ucsf_JAX_NW <- calculate_rank_jaccard(Ucsf_JAX, Ucsf_NW)
ucsf_MSK_UCSF  <- calculate_rank_jaccard(Ucsf_MSK, Ucsf_UCSF)
ucsf_MSK_NW  <- calculate_rank_jaccard(Ucsf_MSK, Ucsf_NW)
ucsf_UCSF_NW <- calculate_rank_jaccard(Ucsf_UCSF, Ucsf_NW)


ucsf_NW_JAX  <- calculate_rank_jaccard(Ucsf_NW, Ucsf_JAX)
ucsf_NW_MSK  <- calculate_rank_jaccard(Ucsf_NW, Ucsf_MSK)
ucsf_NW_UCSF <- calculate_rank_jaccard(Ucsf_NW, Ucsf_UCSF)

ucsf_NW_parent_JAX  <- calculate_rank_jaccard(Ucsf_NW_parent, Ucsf_JAX)
ucsf_NW_parent_MSK  <- calculate_rank_jaccard(Ucsf_NW_parent, Ucsf_MSK)
ucsf_NW_parent_UCSF <- calculate_rank_jaccard(Ucsf_NW_parent, Ucsf_UCSF)

# # Print the results
# cat("Rank-Weighted Jaccard Scores for UCSF:\n")
# cat("JAX vs. MSK: ", ucsf_JAX_MSK, "\n")
# cat("JAX vs. UCSF: ", ucsf_JAX_UCSF, "\n")
# cat("JAX vs. NW:", ucsf_JAX_NW, "\n")
# cat("MSK vs. UCSF:  ", ucsf_MSK_UCSF, "\n")
# cat("MSK vs. NW: ", ucsf_MSK_NW, "\n")
# cat("UCSF vs. NW: ", ucsf_UCSF_NW, "\n")
n_val <- 5000
common_genes <- intersect(Jax_JAX$V1, Jax_MSK$V1) # Get the common background

# 1. Prepare the reference data
df_ref <- Jax_JAX[Jax_JAX$V1 %in% common_genes, ]
# Sort by p-value to identify the "top" genes
df_ref <- df_ref[order(df_ref$adj.P.Val, decreasing = FALSE), ]
# Create a binary outcome: 1 if in the top 500, 0 otherwise
df_ref$is_top <- ifelse(1:nrow(df_ref) <= n_val, 1, 0)

# 2. Fit the logistic regression model
# NOTE: Replace 'AveExpr' with your actual column name for expression level
log_model <- glm(is_top ~ AveExpr, data = df_ref, family = binomial())

# 3. Extract the predicted probabilities to use as sampling weights
gene_probs <- predict(log_model, type = "response")

# 4. Run the expression-weighted simulation
n_simulations <- 100
sim_results <- matrix(NA, nrow = n_simulations, ncol = n_val)

n_val <- 500
for (sim in 1:n_simulations) {
  
  # Sample genes using the logistic regression probabilities as weights
  # This mimics the biological reality that highly expressed genes are drawn more often
  genes_A <- sample(df_ref$V1, size = n_val, replace = FALSE, prob = gene_probs)
  genes_B <- sample(df_ref$V1, size = n_val, replace = FALSE, prob = gene_probs)
  
  # Calculate step-by-step intersection
  intersect_counts <- numeric(n_val)
  for (i in 1:n_val) {
    intersect_counts[i] <- length(intersect(genes_A[1:i], genes_B[1:i]))
  }
  
  # Convert to Jaccard
  union_counts <- (2 * (1:n_val)) - intersect_counts
  sim_results[sim, ] <- intersect_counts / union_counts
}

# 5. Average across simulations and build the null dataframe
expected_jaccard_weighted <- colMeans(sim_results)

null_df <- data.frame(
  Rank = 1:n_val,
  Expected = expected_jaccard_weighted
)

# Load necessary libraries
library(ggplot2)
library(tidyr)
library(gridExtra) # Optional: for putting the two plots side-by-side
## 
## Attaching package: 'gridExtra'
## The following object is masked from 'package:dplyr':
## 
##     combine
## The following object is masked from 'package:Biobase':
## 
##     combine
## The following object is masked from 'package:BiocGenerics':
## 
##     combine
# 1. Prepare the JAX data for plotting
# Create a sequence for the X-axis (Rank 1 to n)
n_val <- length(jax_JAX_MSK) 
rank_seq <- 1:n_val

# Combine the JAX vectors into a single dataframe
jax_df <- data.frame(
  Rank = rank_seq,
  `JAX vs MSK`  = as.numeric(jax_JAX_MSK),
  `JAX vs UCSF` = as.numeric(jax_JAX_UCSF),
  `JAX vs NW`   = as.numeric(jax_JAX_NW),
  `MSK vs UCSF` = as.numeric(jax_MSK_UCSF),
  `MSK vs NW`   = as.numeric(jax_MSK_NW),
  `UCSF vs NW`  = as.numeric(jax_UCSF_NW),
  check.names   = FALSE
)

# Reshape data from "wide" to "long" format for ggplot2
jax_long <- pivot_longer(jax_df, 
                         cols = -Rank, 
                         names_to = "Comparison", 
                         values_to = "Jaccard_Index")

# Create the JAX Plot with Null Baseline
plot_jax <- ggplot(jax_long, aes(x = Rank, y = Jaccard_Index, color = Comparison)) +
  # Add the expected chance dashed line (no CIs)
  geom_line(data = null_df, aes(x = Rank, y = Expected), 
            inherit.aes = FALSE, color = "black", linetype = "dashed", size = 0.8) +
  # Add your actual data lines
  geom_line(size = 1) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Step-by-Step Jaccard Index (JAX Processing)",
       subtitle = "Tracking concordance among the Top 500 genes (Dashed line = Expected by chance)",
       x = "Rank Cutoff (Top i genes)",
       y = "Jaccard Index") +
  theme(legend.position = "right",
        text = element_text(size = 12)) 
## Warning: Using `size` aesthetic for lines was deprecated in ggplot2 3.4.0.
## ℹ Please use `linewidth` instead.
## This warning is displayed once per session.
## Call `lifecycle::last_lifecycle_warnings()` to see where this warning was
## generated.
  # ylim(0, 1) removed to allow the plot to auto-scale


n_val <- 5000
common_genes <- intersect(Ucsf_JAX$V1, Ucsf_MSK$V1) # Get the common background

# 1. Prepare the reference data
df_ref <- Ucsf_JAX[Ucsf_JAX$V1 %in% common_genes, ]
# Sort by p-value to identify the "top" genes
df_ref <- df_ref[order(df_ref$adj.P.Val, decreasing = FALSE), ]
# Create a binary outcome: 1 if in the top 500, 0 otherwise
df_ref$is_top <- ifelse(1:nrow(df_ref) <= n_val, 1, 0)

# 2. Fit the logistic regression model
# NOTE: Replace 'AveExpr' with your actual column name for expression level
log_model <- glm(is_top ~ AveExpr, data = df_ref, family = binomial())

# 3. Extract the predicted probabilities to use as sampling weights
gene_probs <- predict(log_model, type = "response")

# 4. Run the expression-weighted simulation
n_simulations <- 100
sim_results <- matrix(NA, nrow = n_simulations, ncol = n_val)

n_val <- 500
for (sim in 1:n_simulations) {
  
  # Sample genes using the logistic regression probabilities as weights
  # This mimics the biological reality that highly expressed genes are drawn more often
  genes_A <- sample(df_ref$V1, size = n_val, replace = FALSE, prob = gene_probs)
  genes_B <- sample(df_ref$V1, size = n_val, replace = FALSE, prob = gene_probs)
  
  # Calculate step-by-step intersection
  intersect_counts <- numeric(n_val)
  for (i in 1:n_val) {
    intersect_counts[i] <- length(intersect(genes_A[1:i], genes_B[1:i]))
  }
  
  # Convert to Jaccard
  union_counts <- (2 * (1:n_val)) - intersect_counts
  sim_results[sim, ] <- intersect_counts / union_counts
}

# 5. Average across simulations and build the null dataframe
expected_jaccard_weighted <- colMeans(sim_results)

null_df <- data.frame(
  Rank = 1:n_val,
  Expected = expected_jaccard_weighted
)

# 2. Prepare the UCSF data for plotting
ucsf_df <- data.frame(
  Rank = rank_seq,
  `JAX vs MSK`  = as.numeric(ucsf_JAX_MSK),
  `JAX vs UCSF` = as.numeric(ucsf_JAX_UCSF),
  `JAX vs NW`   = as.numeric(ucsf_JAX_NW),
  `MSK vs UCSF` = as.numeric(ucsf_MSK_UCSF),
  `MSK vs NW`   = as.numeric(ucsf_MSK_NW),
  `UCSF vs NW`  = as.numeric(ucsf_UCSF_NW),
  check.names   = FALSE
)

ucsf_long <- pivot_longer(ucsf_df, 
                          cols = -Rank, 
                          names_to = "Comparison", 
                          values_to = "Jaccard_Index")

# Create the UCSF Plot with Null Baseline
plot_ucsf <- ggplot(ucsf_long, aes(x = Rank, y = Jaccard_Index, color = Comparison)) +
  # Add the expected chance dashed line (no CIs)
  geom_line(data = null_df, aes(x = Rank, y = Expected), 
            inherit.aes = FALSE, color = "black", linetype = "dashed", size = 0.8) +
  # Add your actual data lines
  geom_line(size = 1) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Step-by-Step Jaccard Index (UCSF Processing)",
       subtitle = "Tracking concordance among the Top 500 genes (Dashed line = Expected by chance)",
       x = "Rank Cutoff (Top i genes)",
       y = "Jaccard Index") +
  theme(legend.position = "right",
        text = element_text(size = 12)) 
  # ylim(0, 1) removed to allow the plot to auto-scale

# 3. Display the plots
# Show them one by one:
print(plot_jax)

print(plot_ucsf)

# OR show them side-by-side using gridExtra:
# grid.arrange(plot_jax, plot_ucsf, ncol = 2)
# ---- Parameters ----
width_px  <- 1600
height_px <- 1400
ggsave(
  filename = file.path(personal_path, paste0("publication/", "UCSF_Jaccard.png")),
  plot     = plot_ucsf,
  device   = ragg::agg_png,
  bg       = "transparent",
  width    = width_px,
  height   = height_px,
  units    = "px",
  dpi      = 300
)
ggsave(
  filename = file.path(personal_path, paste0("publication/", "JAX_Jaccard.png")),
  plot     = plot_jax,
  device   = ragg::agg_png,
  bg       = "transparent",
  width    = width_px,
  height   = height_px,
  units    = "px",
  dpi      = 300
)
# ==============================================================================
# 1. NW vs. Others (UCSF Processing)
# ==============================================================================

# Prepare the NW data for plotting
nw_df <- data.frame(
  Rank = rank_seq,
  `NW vs JAX`  = as.numeric(ucsf_NW_JAX),
  `NW vs MSK`  = as.numeric(ucsf_NW_MSK),
  `NW vs UCSF` = as.numeric(ucsf_NW_UCSF),
  check.names  = FALSE
)

# Reshape from wide to long
nw_long <- pivot_longer(nw_df, 
                        cols = -Rank, 
                        names_to = "Comparison", 
                        values_to = "Jaccard_Index")

# Create the NW Plot
plot_nw <- ggplot(nw_long, aes(x = Rank, y = Jaccard_Index, color = Comparison)) +
  geom_line(data = null_df, aes(x = Rank, y = Expected), 
            inherit.aes = FALSE, color = "black", linetype = "dashed", size = 0.8) +
  geom_line(size = 1) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Step-by-Step Jaccard Index: NW vs. Others",
       subtitle = "UCSF Processing (Dashed line = Expected by chance)",
       x = "Rank Cutoff (Top i genes)",
       y = "Jaccard Index") +
  theme(legend.position = "right",
        text = element_text(size = 12))

# ==============================================================================
# 2. NW_parent vs. Others (UCSF Processing)
# ==============================================================================

# Prepare the NW_parent data for plotting
nw_parent_df <- data.frame(
  Rank = rank_seq,
  `NW_parent vs JAX`  = as.numeric(ucsf_NW_parent_JAX),
  `NW_parent vs MSK`  = as.numeric(ucsf_NW_parent_MSK),
  `NW_parent vs UCSF` = as.numeric(ucsf_NW_parent_UCSF),
  check.names  = FALSE
)

# Reshape from wide to long
nw_parent_long <- pivot_longer(nw_parent_df, 
                               cols = -Rank, 
                               names_to = "Comparison", 
                               values_to = "Jaccard_Index")

# Create the NW_parent Plot
plot_nw_parent <- ggplot(nw_parent_long, aes(x = Rank, y = Jaccard_Index, color = Comparison)) +
  geom_line(data = null_df, aes(x = Rank, y = Expected), 
            inherit.aes = FALSE, color = "black", linetype = "dashed", size = 0.8) +
  geom_line(size = 1) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Step-by-Step Jaccard Index: NW_parent vs. Others",
       subtitle = "UCSF Processing (Dashed line = Expected by chance)",
       x = "Rank Cutoff (Top i genes)",
       y = "Jaccard Index") +
  theme(legend.position = "right",
        text = element_text(size = 12))

# ==============================================================================
# 3. Display the Plots
# ==============================================================================

print(plot_nw)

print(plot_nw_parent)

# ---- Parameters ----
width_px  <- 1600
height_px <- 1400
ggsave(
  filename = file.path(personal_path, paste0("publication/", "NW_Jaccard.png")),
  plot     = plot_nw,
  device   = ragg::agg_png,
  bg       = "transparent",
  width    = width_px,
  height   = height_px,
  units    = "px",
  dpi      = 300
)
ggsave(
  filename = file.path(personal_path, paste0("publication/", "NW_parent_Jaccard.png")),
  plot     = plot_nw_parent,
  device   = ragg::agg_png,
  bg       = "transparent",
  width    = width_px,
  height   = height_px,
  units    = "px",
  dpi      = 300
)

# Or, if you want to view them side-by-side:
# grid.arrange(plot_nw, plot_nw_parent, ncol = 2)
# Load necessary libraries
library(ggplot2)
library(patchwork) # for arranging plots side-by-side
## 
## Attaching package: 'patchwork'
## The following object is masked from 'package:MASS':
## 
##     area
# 1. Keep your existing functions
score_p_uniform <- function(src_genes, tt_target) {
  idx <- intersect(src_genes, rownames(tt_target))
  if (length(idx) == 0) return(NA_real_)
  p <- tt_target[idx, "P.Value"]           
  p <- p[is.finite(p)]
  if (length(p) == 0) return(NA_real_)
  1 - 2 * mean(p > 0.5)
}

compare_top_n_dpc <- function(df_list, n = 500) {
  labs <- names(df_list)
  prop_mat <- matrix(NA_real_, nrow = length(labs), ncol = length(labs), dimnames = list(labs, labs))
  
  for (i in labs) {
    for (j in labs) {
      if (i == j) {
        prop_mat[i, j] <- 1  
      } else {
        src_df <- df_list[[i]]
        top_n_genes <- rownames(src_df)[order(src_df$P.Value, decreasing = FALSE)][1:n]
        prop_mat[i, j] <- score_p_uniform(top_n_genes, df_list[[j]])
      }
    }
  }
  return(prop_mat)
}

# 2. Group data by pipeline
jax_pipeline_list <- list(JAX = Jax_JAX, MSK = Jax_MSK, UCSF = Jax_UCSF, NW = Jax_NW)
ucsf_pipeline_list <- list(JAX = Ucsf_JAX, MSK = Ucsf_MSK, UCSF = Ucsf_UCSF, NW = Ucsf_NW)

# 3. Define a helper function to create a clean ggplot heatmap
create_heatmap <- function(mat, title) {
  # Convert the matrix into a data frame for ggplot
  df <- as.data.frame(as.table(mat))
  colnames(df) <- c("Source", "Target", "Score")
  
  # Reverse the Source factor levels so the matrix plots top-to-bottom
  df$Source <- factor(df$Source, levels = rev(unique(df$Source)))
  
  ggplot(df, aes(x = Target, y = Source, fill = Score)) +
    geom_tile(color = "white", size = 0.5) +
    # Add text labels (rounded to 2 decimal places)
    geom_text(aes(label = sprintf("%.2f", Score)), color = "black", size = 3.5) +
    # Score logic: theoretical max is 1, theoretical min is -1, null is 0
    scale_fill_gradient2(low = "dodgerblue", mid = "white", high = "firebrick", 
                         midpoint = 0, limits = c(-1, 1), na.value = "grey90",
                         name = "Score") +
    theme_minimal() +
    labs(title = title, x = "Target", y = "Source (Top N)") +
    theme(
      axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, face = "bold"),
      axis.text.y = element_text(face = "bold"),
      panel.grid = element_blank(),
      plot.title = element_text(size = 11, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5)
    )
}

# 4. Generate the matrices and store the plots
n_values <- c(100, 300, 500)
jax_plots <- list()
ucsf_plots <- list()

for (n in n_values) {
  # Calculate matrices
  mat_jax  <- compare_top_n_dpc(jax_pipeline_list, n = n)
  mat_ucsf <- compare_top_n_dpc(ucsf_pipeline_list, n = n)
  
  # Create plots
  jax_plots[[as.character(n)]]  <- create_heatmap(mat_jax, paste("JAX: Top", n))
  ucsf_plots[[as.character(n)]] <- create_heatmap(mat_ucsf, paste("UCSF: Top", n))
}

# 5. Display the plots side-by-side using the 'patchwork' package
# Use the "+" operator to stick them next to each other
jax_combined <- jax_plots[["100"]] + jax_plots[["300"]] + jax_plots[["500"]] + 
                plot_layout(guides = "collect") + 
                plot_annotation(title = "JAX")

ucsf_combined <- ucsf_plots[["100"]] + ucsf_plots[["300"]] + ucsf_plots[["500"]] + 
                 plot_layout(guides = "collect") + 
                 plot_annotation(title = "UCSF")

# Print them to your viewer
print(jax_combined)

print(ucsf_combined)

library(ggplot2)

# 1. Helper function to extract target p-values for the top N source genes
get_target_pvals <- function(src_df, target_df, n = 500) {
  # Get the top N genes from the source by sorting P.Value
  top_genes <- rownames(src_df)[order(src_df$P.Value, decreasing = FALSE)][1:n]
  
  # Find which of those genes exist in the target dataset
  idx <- intersect(top_genes, rownames(target_df))
  
  # Extract the nominal p-values from the target dataset
  pvals <- target_df[idx, "P.Value"]
  pvals <- pvals[is.finite(pvals)] # Clean out any NAs
  
  return(pvals)
}

# 2. Extract the p-values for your 6 specific pairs (Assuming the Jax pipeline)
pval_data <- list(
  "MSK -> JAX"  = get_target_pvals(Jax_MSK, Jax_JAX, n = 500),
  "MSK -> UCSF" = get_target_pvals(Jax_MSK, Jax_UCSF, n = 500),
  "MSK -> NW"   = get_target_pvals(Jax_MSK, Jax_NW, n = 500),
  "JAX -> MSK"  = get_target_pvals(Jax_JAX, Jax_MSK, n = 500),
  "JAX -> UCSF" = get_target_pvals(Jax_JAX, Jax_UCSF, n = 500),
  "JAX -> NW"   = get_target_pvals(Jax_JAX, Jax_NW, n = 500)
)

# 3. Combine them into a single data frame for ggplot
# This loops through the list and binds them into a format ggplot loves
plot_df <- do.call(rbind, lapply(names(pval_data), function(pair_name) {
  data.frame(
    Pair = pair_name,
    P_Value = pval_data[[pair_name]]
  )
}))

# Ensure the plots appear in the specific order you requested
plot_df$Pair <- factor(plot_df$Pair, levels = c(
  "MSK -> JAX", "MSK -> UCSF", "MSK -> NW", 
  "JAX -> MSK", "JAX -> UCSF", "JAX -> NW"
))

# 4. Plot the histograms
ggplot(plot_df, aes(x = P_Value)) +
  # Use 20 bins (breaks every 0.05) so the 0.5 threshold from your heuristic is obvious
  geom_histogram(breaks = seq(0, 1, by = 0.05), fill = "dodgerblue", color = "black", alpha = 0.8) +
  # facet_wrap creates the 2x3 grid of individual plots
  facet_wrap(~ Pair, ncol = 3) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "Target P-Value Distributions of Top 500 Source Genes using JAX data",
    x = "Target Nominal P-Value",
    y = "Gene Count"
  ) +
  theme(
    strip.text = element_text(size = 11, face = "bold"),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  )

pval_data <- list(
  "MSK -> JAX"  = get_target_pvals(Ucsf_MSK, Ucsf_JAX, n = 500),
  "MSK -> UCSF" = get_target_pvals(Ucsf_MSK, Ucsf_UCSF, n = 500),
  "MSK -> NW"   = get_target_pvals(Ucsf_MSK, Ucsf_NW, n = 500),
  "JAX -> MSK"  = get_target_pvals(Ucsf_JAX, Ucsf_MSK, n = 500),
  "JAX -> UCSF" = get_target_pvals(Ucsf_JAX, Ucsf_UCSF, n = 500),
  "JAX -> NW"   = get_target_pvals(Ucsf_JAX, Ucsf_NW, n = 500)
)

# 3. Combine them into a single data frame for ggplot
# This loops through the list and binds them into a format ggplot loves
plot_df <- do.call(rbind, lapply(names(pval_data), function(pair_name) {
  data.frame(
    Pair = pair_name,
    P_Value = pval_data[[pair_name]]
  )
}))

# Ensure the plots appear in the specific order you requested
plot_df$Pair <- factor(plot_df$Pair, levels = c(
  "MSK -> JAX", "MSK -> UCSF", "MSK -> NW", 
  "JAX -> MSK", "JAX -> UCSF", "JAX -> NW"
))

# 4. Plot the histograms
ggplot(plot_df, aes(x = P_Value)) +
  # Use 20 bins (breaks every 0.05) so the 0.5 threshold from your heuristic is obvious
  geom_histogram(breaks = seq(0, 1, by = 0.05), fill = "dodgerblue", color = "black", alpha = 0.8) +
  # facet_wrap creates the 2x3 grid of individual plots
  facet_wrap(~ Pair, ncol = 3) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "Target P-Value Distributions of Top 500 Source Genes using UCSF data",
    x = "Target Nominal P-Value",
    y = "Gene Count"
  ) +
  theme(
    strip.text = element_text(size = 11, face = "bold"),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  )

library(ggplot2)

# 1. Extract the p-values using your UCSF pipeline as the source 
# and the Jax pipeline as the target
neg_pvals <- get_target_pvals(src_df = Ucsf_JAX, target_df = Jax_JAX, n = 500)

# 2. Put it into a simple data frame
neg_df <- data.frame(
  Pair = "Ucsf_JAX -> Jax_JAX",
  P_Value = neg_pvals
)

# 3. Generate the single histogram
ggplot(neg_df, aes(x = P_Value)) +
  # Using a different color (darkorange) to distinguish it from the previous set
  geom_histogram(breaks = seq(0, 1, by = 0.05), fill = "darkorange", color = "black", alpha = 0.8) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "Cross-Pipeline Comparison: Ucsf_JAX to Jax_JAX",
    x = "Jax_JAX Nominal P-Value",
    y = "Gene Count"
  ) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(size = 13, face = "bold"),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  )

# ==============================================================================
# 1. HELPER FUNCTIONS & PARAMETERS
# ==============================================================================

# Set n (Change this to 500 later to answer the question about top 500 genes)
n = 2000

# Helper function to calculate Odds (sig / non-sig) from a vector of p-values
calculate_odds <- function(pvals, thresh = 0.05) {
  sig <- sum(pvals < thresh, na.rm = TRUE)
  ns  <- sum(pvals >= thresh, na.rm = TRUE)
  if (ns == 0) return(NA) # Prevent division by zero
  return(sig / ns)
}

# Helper function to extract target p-values for the top N source genes
get_target_pvals <- function(src_df, target_df, n = 500) {
  # Get the top N genes from the source by sorting P.Value
  top_genes <- rownames(src_df)[order(src_df$P.Value, decreasing = FALSE)][1:n]
  
  # Find which of those genes exist in the target dataset
  idx <- intersect(top_genes, rownames(target_df))
  
  # Extract the nominal p-values from the target dataset
  pvals <- target_df[idx, "P.Value"]
  pvals <- pvals[is.finite(pvals)] # Clean out any NAs
  
  return(pvals)
}

# Helper function to calculate the proportion of nulls
# Simple estimate: 2 * (proportion of p-values > 0.5)
calculate_null_prop <- function(pvals) {
  if(length(pvals) == 0) return(NA)
  null_prop <- 2 * mean(pvals > 0.5, na.rm = TRUE)
  return(min(null_prop, 1)) # cap at 1 (100% null)
}


# ==============================================================================
# 2. JAX DATA PROCESSING
# ==============================================================================

# Extract the 6 lists of p-values for JAX
pval_data_jax <- list(
  "MSK -> JAX"  = get_target_pvals(Jax_MSK, Jax_JAX, n = n),
  "MSK -> UCSF" = get_target_pvals(Jax_MSK, Jax_UCSF, n = n),
  "MSK -> NW"   = get_target_pvals(Jax_MSK, Jax_NW, n = n),
  "JAX -> MSK"  = get_target_pvals(Jax_JAX, Jax_MSK, n = n),
  "JAX -> UCSF" = get_target_pvals(Jax_JAX, Jax_UCSF, n = n),
  "JAX -> NW"   = get_target_pvals(Jax_JAX, Jax_NW, n = n)
)

# Calculate SUBSET Odds (Top n genes)
odds_msk_jax  <- calculate_odds(pval_data_jax[["MSK -> JAX"]])
odds_msk_ucsf <- calculate_odds(pval_data_jax[["MSK -> UCSF"]])
odds_msk_nw   <- calculate_odds(pval_data_jax[["MSK -> NW"]])
odds_jax_msk  <- calculate_odds(pval_data_jax[["JAX -> MSK"]])
odds_jax_ucsf <- calculate_odds(pval_data_jax[["JAX -> UCSF"]])
odds_jax_nw   <- calculate_odds(pval_data_jax[["JAX -> NW"]])

# Calculate BACKGROUND Odds (All genes in the target dataset)
bg_odds_jax_target_jax  <- calculate_odds(Jax_JAX$P.Value)
bg_odds_jax_target_ucsf <- calculate_odds(Jax_UCSF$P.Value)
bg_odds_jax_target_nw   <- calculate_odds(Jax_NW$P.Value)
bg_odds_jax_target_msk  <- calculate_odds(Jax_MSK$P.Value)

# Calculate SUBSET Proportion of Nulls
null_prop_msk_jax  <- calculate_null_prop(pval_data_jax[["MSK -> JAX"]])
null_prop_msk_ucsf <- calculate_null_prop(pval_data_jax[["MSK -> UCSF"]])
null_prop_msk_nw   <- calculate_null_prop(pval_data_jax[["MSK -> NW"]])
null_prop_jax_msk  <- calculate_null_prop(pval_data_jax[["JAX -> MSK"]])
null_prop_jax_ucsf <- calculate_null_prop(pval_data_jax[["JAX -> UCSF"]])
null_prop_jax_nw   <- calculate_null_prop(pval_data_jax[["JAX -> NW"]])

# Calculate BACKGROUND Proportion of Nulls (All genes)
bg_null_prop_jax_target_jax  <- calculate_null_prop(Jax_JAX$P.Value)
bg_null_prop_jax_target_ucsf <- calculate_null_prop(Jax_UCSF$P.Value)
bg_null_prop_jax_target_nw   <- calculate_null_prop(Jax_NW$P.Value)
bg_null_prop_jax_target_msk  <- calculate_null_prop(Jax_MSK$P.Value)

# Create the Final Table for JAX
results_table_jax <- data.frame(
  Comparison = c("JAX -> UCSF", "JAX -> NW", "JAX -> MSK", "MSK -> UCSF", "MSK -> NW", "MSK -> JAX"),
  Null_Proportion_Subset = c(null_prop_jax_ucsf, null_prop_jax_nw, null_prop_jax_msk, null_prop_msk_ucsf, null_prop_msk_nw, null_prop_msk_jax),
  Null_Proportion_Background = c(bg_null_prop_jax_target_ucsf, bg_null_prop_jax_target_nw, bg_null_prop_jax_target_msk, bg_null_prop_jax_target_ucsf, bg_null_prop_jax_target_nw, bg_null_prop_jax_target_jax),
  Subset_Odds = c(odds_jax_ucsf, odds_jax_nw, odds_jax_msk, odds_msk_ucsf, odds_msk_nw, odds_msk_jax),
  Background_Odds = c(bg_odds_jax_target_ucsf, bg_odds_jax_target_nw, bg_odds_jax_target_msk, bg_odds_jax_target_ucsf, bg_odds_jax_target_nw, bg_odds_jax_target_jax),
  Odds_Ratio_Subset_vs_Background = c(
    odds_jax_ucsf / bg_odds_jax_target_ucsf,
    odds_jax_nw   / bg_odds_jax_target_nw,
    odds_jax_msk  / bg_odds_jax_target_msk,
    odds_msk_ucsf / bg_odds_jax_target_ucsf,
    odds_msk_nw   / bg_odds_jax_target_nw,
    odds_msk_jax  / bg_odds_jax_target_jax
  )
)

print("=== METRICS FOR JAX DATA ===")
## [1] "=== METRICS FOR JAX DATA ==="
print(results_table_jax)
##    Comparison Null_Proportion_Subset Null_Proportion_Background Subset_Odds
## 1 JAX -> UCSF                  0.009                  0.2054149    67.96552
## 2   JAX -> NW                  0.033                  0.4364258    11.82051
## 3  JAX -> MSK                  0.001                  0.3689975   284.71429
## 4 MSK -> UCSF                  0.001                  0.2054149   665.66667
## 5   MSK -> NW                  0.037                  0.4364258    13.70588
## 6  MSK -> JAX                  0.000                  0.3095572  1999.00000
##   Background_Odds Odds_Ratio_Subset_vs_Background
## 1        2.649300                        25.65414
## 2        0.864255                        13.67711
## 3        1.178426                       241.60562
## 4        2.649300                       251.26131
## 5        0.864255                        15.85861
## 6        1.524637                      1311.13206
# ==============================================================================
# 3. UCSF DATA PROCESSING
# ==============================================================================

# Extract the 6 lists of p-values for UCSF
pval_data_ucsf <- list(
  "MSK -> JAX"  = get_target_pvals(Ucsf_MSK, Ucsf_JAX, n = n),
  "MSK -> UCSF" = get_target_pvals(Ucsf_MSK, Ucsf_UCSF, n = n),
  "MSK -> NW"   = get_target_pvals(Ucsf_MSK, Ucsf_NW, n = n),
  "JAX -> MSK"  = get_target_pvals(Ucsf_JAX, Ucsf_MSK, n = n),
  "JAX -> UCSF" = get_target_pvals(Ucsf_JAX, Ucsf_UCSF, n = n),
  "JAX -> NW"   = get_target_pvals(Ucsf_JAX, Ucsf_NW, n = n)
)

# Calculate SUBSET Odds (Renamed variables to avoid overwriting JAX)
odds_msk_jax_ucsf  <- calculate_odds(pval_data_ucsf[["MSK -> JAX"]])
odds_msk_ucsf_ucsf <- calculate_odds(pval_data_ucsf[["MSK -> UCSF"]])
odds_msk_nw_ucsf   <- calculate_odds(pval_data_ucsf[["MSK -> NW"]])
odds_jax_msk_ucsf  <- calculate_odds(pval_data_ucsf[["JAX -> MSK"]])
odds_jax_ucsf_ucsf <- calculate_odds(pval_data_ucsf[["JAX -> UCSF"]])
odds_jax_nw_ucsf   <- calculate_odds(pval_data_ucsf[["JAX -> NW"]])

# Calculate BACKGROUND Odds (All genes in the target dataset)
bg_odds_ucsf_target_jax  <- calculate_odds(Ucsf_JAX$P.Value)
bg_odds_ucsf_target_ucsf <- calculate_odds(Ucsf_UCSF$P.Value)
bg_odds_ucsf_target_nw   <- calculate_odds(Ucsf_NW$P.Value)
bg_odds_ucsf_target_msk  <- calculate_odds(Ucsf_MSK$P.Value)

# Calculate SUBSET Proportion of Nulls
null_prop_msk_jax_ucsf  <- calculate_null_prop(pval_data_ucsf[["MSK -> JAX"]])
null_prop_msk_ucsf_ucsf <- calculate_null_prop(pval_data_ucsf[["MSK -> UCSF"]])
null_prop_msk_nw_ucsf   <- calculate_null_prop(pval_data_ucsf[["MSK -> NW"]])
null_prop_jax_msk_ucsf  <- calculate_null_prop(pval_data_ucsf[["JAX -> MSK"]])
null_prop_jax_ucsf_ucsf <- calculate_null_prop(pval_data_ucsf[["JAX -> UCSF"]])
null_prop_jax_nw_ucsf   <- calculate_null_prop(pval_data_ucsf[["JAX -> NW"]])

# Calculate BACKGROUND Proportion of Nulls 
bg_null_prop_ucsf_target_jax  <- calculate_null_prop(Ucsf_JAX$P.Value)
bg_null_prop_ucsf_target_ucsf <- calculate_null_prop(Ucsf_UCSF$P.Value)
bg_null_prop_ucsf_target_nw   <- calculate_null_prop(Ucsf_NW$P.Value)
bg_null_prop_ucsf_target_msk  <- calculate_null_prop(Ucsf_MSK$P.Value)

# Create the Final Table for UCSF
results_table_ucsf <- data.frame(
  Comparison = c("JAX -> UCSF", "JAX -> NW", "JAX -> MSK", "MSK -> UCSF", "MSK -> NW", "MSK -> JAX"),
  Null_Proportion_Subset = c(null_prop_jax_ucsf_ucsf, null_prop_jax_nw_ucsf, null_prop_jax_msk_ucsf, null_prop_msk_ucsf_ucsf, null_prop_msk_nw_ucsf, null_prop_msk_jax_ucsf),
  Null_Proportion_Background = c(bg_null_prop_ucsf_target_ucsf, bg_null_prop_ucsf_target_nw, bg_null_prop_ucsf_target_msk, bg_null_prop_ucsf_target_ucsf, bg_null_prop_ucsf_target_nw, bg_null_prop_ucsf_target_jax),
  Subset_Odds = c(odds_jax_ucsf_ucsf, odds_jax_nw_ucsf, odds_jax_msk_ucsf, odds_msk_ucsf_ucsf, odds_msk_nw_ucsf, odds_msk_jax_ucsf),
  Background_Odds = c(bg_odds_ucsf_target_ucsf, bg_odds_ucsf_target_nw, bg_odds_ucsf_target_msk, bg_odds_ucsf_target_ucsf, bg_odds_ucsf_target_nw, bg_odds_ucsf_target_jax),
  Odds_Ratio_Subset_vs_Background = c(
    odds_jax_ucsf_ucsf / bg_odds_ucsf_target_ucsf,
    odds_jax_nw_ucsf   / bg_odds_ucsf_target_nw,
    odds_jax_msk_ucsf  / bg_odds_ucsf_target_msk,
    odds_msk_ucsf_ucsf / bg_odds_ucsf_target_ucsf,
    odds_msk_nw_ucsf   / bg_odds_ucsf_target_nw,
    odds_msk_jax_ucsf  / bg_odds_ucsf_target_jax
  )
)

print("=== METRICS FOR UCSF DATA ===")
## [1] "=== METRICS FOR UCSF DATA ==="
print(results_table_ucsf)

## 4 MSK -> UCSF              0.3493976                  0.6434881   1.0020101
## 5   MSK -> NW              0.5000000                  0.7463483   0.5910543
## 6  MSK -> JAX              0.3677582                  0.5894413   0.9479882

## 1       0.3099971                        9.056919
